name: gitlab
context: GitLab ecosystem for projects, issues, merge requests, pipelines, CI/CD variables, and API-driven engineering automation
info: GitLab is an integrated software delivery platform that combines source control, issue tracking, merge requests, CI/CD pipelines, project settings, and token-based API access behind one host. Autonomous agents repeatedly use GitLab to create or inspect projects, file issues, open and merge merge requests, trigger or retry pipelines, and manage CI/CD variables across GitLab.com and self-managed instances. The execution surface is primarily the REST API under `/api/v4`, the `.gitlab-ci.yml` configuration model, and the official `glab` CLI.
body:
# GitLab Ecosystem

GitLab exposes most engineering workflows through one host: repositories, issues, merge requests, CI/CD pipelines, runners, and project configuration. For automation, the most important surface is the REST API at `https://<host>/api/v4`, where `id` parameters usually accept either a numeric project ID or a URL-encoded `group/project` path.

```bash
curl --header 'PRIVATE-TOKEN: $GITLAB_TOKEN' \
  'https://gitlab.com/api/v4/projects?membership=true&search=platform'
```

Load [[gitlab/auth]] first to choose the right token and host model. Load [[gitlab/ci]] when pipeline behavior depends on `.gitlab-ci.yml`, runner availability, variables, or typed pipeline inputs. The executable skills in this tree are organized around the jobs agents actually perform: [[gitlab/projects]], [[gitlab/issues]], [[gitlab/merge-requests]], [[gitlab/pipelines]], and [[gitlab/variables]].

sections.title: API surface and host model
sections.body: GitLab automation usually targets the REST API under `/api/v4`. On GitLab.com the base is `https://gitlab.com/api/v4`; on self-managed or Dedicated instances it is `https://<your-host>/api/v4`. Most project endpoints accept a numeric project ID or a URL-encoded path like `group%2Fproject`. Pagination is commonly controlled with `page` and `per_page`, and some high-volume project listings also support keyset pagination.

sections.title: Primary agent jobs
sections.body: The recurring jobs for agents are: create or inspect projects, keep project settings aligned with branch and CI rules, file or close issues, open merge requests from prepared branches, merge with SHA guards or auto-merge, trigger pipelines with variables or typed inputs, and manage project-scoped CI/CD variables for deploys. These jobs map directly to [[gitlab/projects]], [[gitlab/issues]], [[gitlab/merge-requests]], [[gitlab/pipelines]], and [[gitlab/variables]].

sections.title: GitLab.com versus self-managed
sections.body: The same API shape works across GitLab.com, GitLab Self-Managed, and GitLab Dedicated, but hostname, SSO requirements, runner topology, and feature availability can differ. In automation, always make the host explicit with `GITLAB_HOST`, avoid assuming `gitlab.com`, and prefer URL-encoded full project paths when the numeric ID is not known yet. See [[gitlab/auth#glab]] for multi-host CLI patterns.
